PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
WHAT DO I NEED TO MEASURE?
The theory behind measuring/metrics is that you want to measure the
behaviour of your prospects and look for the implication of those behaviors so
you can improve things. For example, if you can see a pattern related to why
prospects don’t buy, that will help you to know what to change to improve
conversions.
The only way you can get the information on what to change is to track lots of
things. In general you want to look for underperforming statistics such as an
email with an unusually low (relative to your other emails) click through rate.
Some of the most important things to measure are registration rates,
attendance rates and purchase rates.
Keep in mind that data becomes more valuable:
✓ 	When it’s accurate
✓ 	When you have more of it
✓ 	When it’s confirmed
For more on this topic, see the
Webinar Metrics Checklist

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
HOW CAN I USE WEBINARS TO TEST A
NEW PRODUCT ?
Have a new product idea? Try running a non-selling webinar on the subject
to test interest. With a day’s worth of work you can see whether you’ve got
enough interest to make the product worth your while.
When running a webinar as a testing tool, the idea is to not sell anything in
that webinar. Instead, the purpose is to see if they’re interested before trying
to sell them anything (if they are interested, then you can use another
webinar to sell).
THE EMAIL SEQUENCE
✓ 	2-3 emails establishing the problem and providing small tactical solutions
✓ 	Announce and promote the webinar to help them solve it
✓ 	People who sign up get tagged with an “interest” tag
FOR THE 70% WHO DIDN'T REGISTER FOR THE WEBINAR…
There are two determiners as to whether or not they may be interested in
your offer:
1. 	Did they click on any of the emails in the sequence?
2. 	Ask them… “Are you interested in knowing more about solving x
problem? Yes/No?”
What do you do with the results?
✓ 	Tag them appropriately
✓ 	If they express an interest, they get the full promotional sequence
✓ 	If they do not express an interest, move them on to the next product
sequence
✓ 	If they don’t answer (or don't open any of the emails), look at clicks (if
they’re not opening, not clicking and didn’t answer, that’s an indication
that they aren’t interested and you should move them on to the next
product in the sequence)

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
WHAT IS THE MINIMUM I NEED TO HAVE
IN PL ACE FOR A VIABLE WEBINAR AND
WEBINAR FUNNEL?
The minimum viable promotion is based on the concept of the minimum
viable product; that version of a new product which allows you to collect the
maximum amount of validated learning about your customers and
marketing with the least amount of effort.
You’re trying to spend the least amount of effort and time to test whether
your idea will work, before you put the rest of the work in.
THE MINIMUM VIABLE WEBINAR
The minimum viable webinar lets you learn about your webinar content, your
offer, your audience (and lets you sell), with the least amount of effort. Here,
the foundation of a webinar is created in a short period of time (it’s the
psychology and incorporating case studies/testimonials that takes time and is
held for later).
To create your minimum viable webinar:
✓ 	Follow the outline to build the basic framework for your webinar (Modules
4 and 5) but don't build in the multiple layers of psychology (testimonials,
case studies, seeding, social proof, perfecting your pivot)
✓ 	Once you have the basic webinar in place, deliver it, and pay special
attention to:
• Questions and chat (what didn’t they understand)?
• Objections (what needs to be more convincing)?
• View times (when did people leave the webinar)?
✓ 	Focus on improving those areas that need work
✓ 	Deliver the webinar again, and repeat the steps above
This will get you to a converting webinar faster and with less effort.
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	6 	17
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 17 --

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
The challenge here is that people don’t want to watch a webinar more than
once. So every person who watches and doesn’t buy is a lost opportunity (at
least for awhile). This is made worse by running a webinar to an existing list
(i.e. the best potential customers). Ideally you want the best version of your
webinar right out of the gate but that’s not always realistic so consider
starting out with paid traffic; refining your webinar with those prospects,
then expose your webinar to your existing list once you’ve got it dialed in.
Alternatively, run the webinar to those who have been on your list for awhile
but haven’t bought yet.
THE MINIMUM VIABLE FUNNEL
If you're trying to get your funnel up and running as fast as possible, consider
removing the Sales Page Promo from your funnel. Here’s a look at the two
funnels:
The Fully-Developed Funnel
What You Can Remove From Your Funnel

earlier in the course. Deliver your webinar, analyze it, tweak it, repeat!

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
HOW AND WHY SHOULD I CONSIDER
HOSTING LIVE BROADCASTS OF MY
WEBINAR?
A livecast is more like a TV broadcast than a traditional slide-based
webinar. They can be done alone (think of Jeff Walker’s live launch), or with
guests (a mixture of celebrity guests giving testimonials); either live on set or
live online (via Skype or Zoom).
They can be extravagant, multi-hour events that create massive positive
engagement. Often they have different segments, periodic prices, a
dedicated set, multiple mailings during the event to announce segments.
Livecasts are much more complex. There is a higher potential for things to go
sideways. You only want to do a livecast if you’re going to have a large
audience (but it can also be the thing that creates a large audience).
The key is to making sure that it sells; you do that by interspersing the livecast
with regular offers.
USING FACEBOOK AND YOUTUBE LIVE
Consider Facebook and YouTube Live for your livecast as they have large built
in audiences. They also give your prospects the option to turn on notifications
(to get alerts when you go live).
You can consider hosting your webinar exclusively on these platforms. This
can work when:
✓ 	You have an audience that you already communicate with primarily on
that platform
✓ 	You already use streamed video to communicate with that audience
✓ 	The audience is substantial
✓ 	You’ve sold to that audience this way before
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	9 	17

-- 9 of 17 --

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
Facebook/Youtube Live Promo Model
This is the promotional model to use if you’re going to do a livecast:
Regular Live Streaming Video Before the Launch
✓ 	Usually face-to-camera (not screen capture)
✓ 	Start talking about the sales “event” days/weeks in advance
✓ 	The event is turned into something special that people are anticipating as
it approaches
Sales Event
✓ 	Not just one webinar, a series of streamed videos that sell
✓ 	Pre-announced time for at least the launch broadcast
✓ 	Usually face-to-camera
✓ 	Much more relationship-based sales process
✓ 	Encourage and reward engagement via prizes and play games in the prize
giveaway
Return to Regular Non-Sales Live Streaming Videos
✓ 	Post-sales event, return to regular broadcasts

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
WHAT IS A JV WEBINAR AND HOW DO I
GET PARTNERS TO PROMOTE FOR ME?
JV stands for Joint Venture. A JV webinar is where someone else (a JV
partner) sends traffic to a webinar that you are delivering. The JV uses a
special URL that tracks the traffic as coming from that particular JV partner.
You pay them a commission on sales that come from the people they send to
your webinar.
You can have multiple partners on a webinar but it’s not as effective; we
recommend one partner at a time. This way, the JV partner hosts the webinar,
personalizing it to his/her audience, and offers a bonus as part of your offer.
JV webinars can:
• Happen any time, whether you’re in launch mode or not
• Happen without you needing to mail your list
• Bring in income consistently throughout the year
• “Introduce” your offering to a JV partner (so they’ll want to promote your
next launch)
• Significantly build your list and impact
• Bring in significant amounts of income
For more on this topic, see the
JV Webinar Checklist

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
HOW DO I PUT MY WEBINAR ON
AUTOPILOT ?
Evergreen Webinars are a powerful way to grow your webinar business. The
key to a profitable Evergreen Webinar is to add more trust elements to
the funnel; warming up cold traffic before you make an offer to them.
Evergreen Webinars do require work and testing. In general, we suggest that
you don’t go evergreen until you’ve presented your webinar live at least 20
times (this is the 20X rule of evergreen). During this time you want to record,
analyze and improve your webinars.
There are six things you need to do to put your webinar on autopilot:
✓ 	Build your funnel
✓ 	Prove your funnel
✓ 	Prove your recording
✓ 	Switch to variable times
✓ 	Scale your traffic
✓ 	Conduct ongoing maintenance
For more on this topic, see the
Evergreen Webinar Checklist
HYBRID EVERGREEN WEBINARS
Hybrid Evergreen Webinars are a combination of recording and live
performance. The webinar runs at a certain time, prospects watch a
recording, then you come on to the webinar live at the end to answer
questions.
This model lets you retain the power of the live questions and the trust that’s
built through your answers.
This is a great option for the early days of your Evergreen Webinar so you can
figure out what’s working and what’s not (by the questions they’re asking and
how you’re converting).
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	13 	17
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 17 --

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
There are two styles of Hybrid Evergreen Webinars:
• Invisible: The attendees have no idea what’s live and what’s not
• Explicit: You tell attendees exactly what’s going on

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
WHAT ARE SOME OTHER WAYS I CAN
BUILD MY BUSINESS WITH WEBINARS?
In addition to the strategies above, there are four additional ways you can
build and diversify your business with webinars:
• Webinars for other peoples’ products
• Sponsored webinars
• Webinar funnel coach or consultant
• Different hook, same offer webinars
WEBINARS FOR OTHER PEOPLES’ PRODUCTS
The concept here is that you provide the marketing/webinar; they provide the
product. For example, you host a webinar for someone else’s product (where
they present an offer to your audience) and you provide a recommendation,
“I’ve seen the product he/she is going to offer you at the end of this webinar,
and I recommend you buy it” (you can also add your own bonus to the offer).
There are two options for how to do this:
✓ 	Option 1: You interview them… they present the material, you guide them
through the process through your interview and inject psychology
(consider doing this with a script)
✓ 	Option 2: You run your own webinars without them… you control
everything except the sales page and can even build out your own
webinar funnel
You may want to consider a webinar for other people’s products when:
✓ 	There is a high commission involved
✓ 	You have a pre-existing knowledge of the subject matter
✓ 	Their offer is weak or not targeted to your audience
✓ 	They’re great product creators, but not so good at marketing
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	15 	17
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
SPONSORED WEBINARS & COACHING/CONSULTING
There are a lot of businesses that have their own sales systems in place to
close sales that could gain value from a great webinar and funnel. There are
several options to consider to generate revenue from selling services to these
businesses:
Option 1. Sell a complete webinar funnel
Option 2. Sell a webinar without the sales offer portion
✓ 	The webinar contains the entire content portion, some of the offer portion,
but doesn’t directly ask for the order (instead, refers people to their
existing closing method or website)
Option 3. Sell a sponsored webinar
✓ 	You deliver the webinar, they sponsor it
✓ 	Usually content-section only
✓ 	No sales message. Think of these like a podcast, radio or TV sponsorship.
No offer, just a “this webinar is sponsored by_________” plus a minute or so
of copy about them
✓ 	This can be marketed to their existing leads and/or paid traffic
A variation on Option 3:
✓ 	You run a webinar to paid traffic
✓ 	Interview them on the subject matter
✓ 	They sponsor the webinar
✓ 	No sales offer, just a message from the sponsor
You can also consider selling webinar funnel creation services:
✓ 	Webinar scripting
✓ 	Webinar presentation
✓ 	Webinar funnel building
✓ 	Webinar advertising services
✓ 	Evergreen Webinar funnel building
✓ 	Webinar conversion improvement consulting
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	16 	17
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 17 --

PLANNER / M8. WEBINAR BUSINESS PLANNER
DIFFERENT HOOK, SAME OFFER WEBINARS
One of the problems with webinars is that most people don’t want to hear
the same message over and over again. You can solve that with different
hook, same offer webinars. This way you can regularly promote the same
product to the same group of people just by changing your hook and
content. Here’s how:
✓ 	Change out the content portion of the webinar to resolve a different, but
related, need
✓ 	Change your webinar marketing to focus on that new need
✓ 	Market the new webinar to the same audience you worked with before
Example: Weight loss
• 	Old message: Dieting
• 	New message: Change your body’s metabolism so you don’t have to diet
• 	Another message: The 5 most effective exercises to lose the most weight
in just 10 minutes a day
